problem_statement: Flight search by origin airport is case-sensitive, so requests like origin=ath do not match stored airport codes such as ATH and return no results.
goal: Make the origin filter in flight search match airport codes regardless of input casing, while preserving existing search behavior for other filters.
acceptance_criteria: GET /flights/search?origin=ath returns flights whose origin airport code is stored as ATH
Origin matching works for lowercase, uppercase, and mixed-case input
No regression in other flight search filters or result ordering
constraints: Airport codes are stored in uppercase
Fix should be localized to the flight search implementation
Affected area is backend/app/services/flight_service.py in search_flights
unknowns: Whether search_flights normalizes only origin or other airport-code filters as well
Whether matching is done in Python after fetching data or in the database query
repo_summary: Backend service for flights is the likely source of the bug; API route exists in backend/app/api/routes/flights.py, but the issue description explicitly points to backend/app/services/flight_service.py search_flights. Related models/schemas/schema files may define flight fields and query shape.

targeted_symbols.reason: The issue explicitly names this function and the symptom is a case-sensitive origin filter. This is the smallest likely implementation area for normalizing the origin input or comparison.

localization_rationale: The bug is directly tied to the origin search parameter, not flight creation or seeding.
The issue description explicitly identifies backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights) as the affected file.
A minimal fix should normalize the origin input or comparison within search_flights rather than changing broader API or model layers.
